steps.title: Identify the alignment between attitude and motto
steps.content: Sentence 1: Montaigne questions his own perspective throughout his essays. Sentence 2: his motto was "What do I know?" — a rhetorical question that directly embodies that questioning attitude. The two fit together naturally.

steps.title: Match the alignment to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is fittingness/confirmation: the motto appropriately exemplifies the trait just described.
  • "Still,": signals a concession — something persists despite prior conditions. No prior obstacle or contrast precedes the motto. ✗
  • "Conversely,": signals an opposite situation. The motto does not contradict his questioning approach; it embodies it. ✗
  • "Fittingly,": signals that what follows is an appropriate or natural match for what was just described. The self-doubting motto is a fitting expression of his questioning mindset. ✓
  • "Nowadays,": signals a shift to the present time. The motto belongs to Montaigne's own era; no time shift is needed or appropriate. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
